task_completion.description: LibreCAD is opened and the required layers are created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD interface is visible. The Layer List panel shows the layers: 'WALLS', 'BORDER', 'TILES', 'DIMENSIONS', and 'TEXT'.

task_completion.description: The outer room perimeter is drawn on the WALLS layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A large rectangle representing the 3000x2400 room is visible in the main drawing canvas.

task_completion.description: The inner border is drawn on the BORDER layer in red.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A red rectangle is drawn inside the outer walls rectangle, with a visible inset gap (representing 150mm) on all sides.

task_completion.description: The tile grid is drawn on the TILES layer in cyan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A grid of cyan lines is visible inside the red border. There should be exactly 8 vertical lines and 6 horizontal lines forming the grid.

task_completion.description: Dimensions for the room width and height are added to the drawing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dimension lines with text showing '3000' (for width) and '2400' (for height) are visible alongside the outer rectangle.

task_completion.description: The required text label is added and the file is saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'RESTROOM TILE LAYOUT - SCALE 1:20' is visible below the drawing. The LibreCAD window title or a save dialog shows the filename 'restroom_tile_layout.dxf'.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD GUI to create the drawing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the LibreCAD application interface with drawing tools, layer panels, and the canvas being actively used, rather than just a terminal window.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script to generate the DXF file directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows do not show Python, Bash, or other scripts being written or executed to output the DXF file without interacting with LibreCAD.

integrity.description: The drawing is constructed using CAD tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD canvas shows selection handles, crosshairs, grid dots, or command line inputs typical of CAD drafting, proving it is not just an image viewer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The drawing must contain exactly 5 specific layers: WALLS, BORDER, TILES, DIMENSIONS, and TEXT. The outer room dimensions (WALLS) are 3000x2400. The inner border (BORDER) is inset by 150mm, making it 2700x2100. The tile grid (TILES) consists of exactly 14 lines total (8 vertical, 6 horizontal) inside the border. The final file must be saved as '/home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/restroom_tile_layout.dxf'.
